preamble: The voiced palatal plosive [ɟ] frequently shifts to the voiced postalveolar fricative [ʒ] through spirantization. In Romance linguistics, this represents a key stage in the development of voiced velars and palatals into the modern French 'j' sound.

examples.note: Latin [g] before front vowels palatalized to [ɟ] and eventually spirantized to [ʒ].
Latin [dj] cluster merged into a palatal plosive [ɟ] stage before reaching [ʒ].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
